description: User directive (verbatim, this session): 'first we need a script that will use nlp processing to pre-identify candidates needing full agent treatment for validating / de-hedging / correcting, for all substrate items, not just gaps.' Implemented as scripts/scan-substrate-hedges.ts (read-only, informational, non-gate; matching this repo's scan-comment-citations.ts precedent): scans EVERY block kind declared in the active substrate's config (config.block_kinds via loadConfig -- no hardcoded block list, per this project's derive-from-registry convention) and every schema-declared prose field of every item (a string property counts as prose iff its schema declares no enum/pattern/format/const -- ids, statuses, dates, hashes all declare one; the walk recurses through arrays-of-strings and arrays-of-objects; x-prompt-budget presence is recorded on findings but not required -- no hardcoded field list). All substrate access flows through the canonical pi-context surface (loadConfig / readSchema / readBlock), never raw fs reads of the substrate's *.json. Heuristic pattern matching in three weighted categories, grounded in the hedges the manual FGAP-124/125/126/127 provenance audits actually confirmed: (1) fork -- enumerated-alternative language (and/or; either...or; dash-or as in FGAP-125's live '-- or amend the schema'; semicolon-or; paren-or and or+gerund as in pre-correction FGAP-126; lettered '(a)...(b)' option lists -- numbered '(1)...(2)' deliberately unmatched because live substrate prose uses it to enumerate facts, not alternatives; 'alternatively'; option-enumeration cues); (2) deferral -- explicit decision-postponement ('as a decision' -- the exact FGAP-126 augmentation phrase -- TBD, to be determined/decided, not yet decided, open question, user's / user scope call, up to the user, undecided/undetermined, deferred, left/remains open, pending/needs a decision, decide later, punt, weak 'not yet'); (3) modal-hedge -- low-weight uncertainty vocabulary (might, could, may need/want/require, possibly/perhaps, probably/presumably, eventually, at some point, unclear/uncertain, unknown, later, seems/appears to, if needed). Every match carries pattern id + category + weight (1-4); an item's score is the summed weight across its fields, candidates rank by score with a min-score threshold (default 2, --min-score). Output: a single machine-readable JSON report (tool / generated_at / minScore / per-block summaries / reconciled totals / candidates sorted by score; per match: field path, matched text, surrounding-context snippet) written to a timestamped path under gitignored tmp/substrate-hedge-scans/ by default; --output overrides the path, --stdout prints the full report instead. The ranked candidate list is what an orchestrator works through with the gap-fork-provenance-audit-brief-template (analysis/2026-07-11-gap-fork-provenance-audit-brief-template.md) or its generalization to other block kinds. Weights rank, they do not judge: grounded forks (FGAP-124's and FGAP-127's were audited and left standing) are still flagged by design -- grounded-vs-invented is exactly what the downstream per-item agent audit determines; recall is deliberately favored over precision. The script itself corrects nothing and makes zero substrate writes -- pre-identification only.
acceptance_criteria: Script discovers block kinds and their schemas dynamically from the active substrate's config (block_kinds, schema field definitions) -- no hardcoded list of block names or field names anywhere in the script.
Scans every schema-declared prose field (string properties without enum/pattern/format/const, including strings nested inside arrays and objects) across every item in every discovered block.

Flags matches for all three marker categories (enumerated-option forks, explicit-deferral phrases, vague/modal hedging), each match carrying pattern id, category, weight, matched text, and a surrounding-context snippet; candidates are ranked by summed match weight with a configurable min-score threshold.

Output is a single machine-readable JSON file at a timestamped path (default under gitignored tmp/substrate-hedge-scans/; --output and --stdout available); the script makes zero substrate writes (read-only).

Running it against the current substrate's framework-gaps block produces a report that includes FGAP-125's still-unaudited proposed_resolution or-clause as a known true-positive sanity check (met at implementation: FGAP-125 flagged on proposed_resolution via the dash-or fork pattern; the test suite pins this conditionally so the cell passes vacuously once the fork is audited away downstream).

Unit tests cover each heuristic marker category against synthetic true-positive and true-negative fixtures, the schema-driven prose-field classification/extraction, per-item aggregation, and live-substrate structural invariants (tsx --test, matching this project's testing convention).
